verses.text: The Philistines set the captured ark beside their idol Dagon as a trophy — and twice find Dagon fallen on his face before it, at last with head and hands broken off. The living God needs no defending; in His presence the idols of the nations simply collapse.

The Philistines set the captured ark beside their idol Dagon as a trophy — and twice find Dagon fallen on his face before it, at last with head and hands broken off. The living God needs no defending; in His presence the idols of the nations simply collapse.

The Philistines set the captured ark beside their idol Dagon as a trophy — and twice find Dagon fallen on his face before it, at last with head and hands broken off. The living God needs no defending; in His presence the idols of the nations simply collapse.

Then the LORD's hand grows heavy on Ashdod, striking the city with tumours. They learn the ark is no harmless prize. 'His hand is sore upon us,' they cry. God may seem captured, but He is never at the mercy of those who think they hold Him.

Then the LORD's hand grows heavy on Ashdod, striking the city with tumours. They learn the ark is no harmless prize. 'His hand is sore upon us,' they cry. God may seem captured, but He is never at the mercy of those who think they hold Him.

They pass the ark to Gath, and the plague follows it there. What they took for a trophy has become a terror they can neither keep nor escape. Sin imagines it has seized God's blessing, only to find it cannot live with what it has grasped.

They pass the ark to Gath, and the plague follows it there. What they took for a trophy has become a terror they can neither keep nor escape. Sin imagines it has seized God's blessing, only to find it cannot live with what it has grasped.

At Ekron the people panic — 'they have brought the ark to slay us' — and the cry of the city goes up to heaven. The God they would not honour they now cannot endure. Far better to fear the LORD in worship than to meet His hand in judgment.
